Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

- Goal model: Advance the white-bordered purple dot along the trail until it reaches the gray-bordered dot at (59,27)
- Plan: Click the magenta dot at (56,27).
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model.

sections.content:
The result says `revisited_state: true` and `state_node_id: 1, state_turn: 2`. This means I've returned to the same state as turn 2 (after the first magenta dot click). So the state is now the same as after the first click on the magenta dot.
Looking at the image, the white-bordered dot is now at (40,33) where the magenta dot was, and there's a new magenta dot further down. Let me check the current state to find the new magenta dot position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Key objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 25 and n['color'] not in ('B', 'g'):
        center_row = sum(b[0] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_col = sum(b[1] for b in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} ~({center_row},{center_col}) children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
